response.justification: Candidate is an accomplished full-stack software craftsman with strong web, mobile, and embedded software experience, but the role requires deep specialization in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware, performance architecture, and hardware-software co-design. The resume shows no evidence of GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture work, deep neural network optimization, or chip-level performance modeling, and lacks the advanced degree typically expected for a Principal Performance Architect position.
strengths: Long track record of senior/staff IC engineering roles across diverse domains
Some low-level systems exposure via C/C++ at National Instruments and DLang high-performance services
Strong engineering discipline (TDD, CI/CD, performance-aware backend work)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator hardware experience
No demonstrated work on deep neural network training/inference optimization
Educational background (BS only) and domain experience do not match a Principal Performance Architect profile

key_factors.factor: Missing hardware/SoC and AI accelerator expertise
No deep-learning workload or GPU architecture background
General software engineering depth and seniority
